title: Rescaffold product pages from Google Stitch HTML prototypes
summary: Three Google Stitch HTML prototypes are available on the local filesystem. These contain production-quality Tailwind CSS layouts with the exact design tokens, typography, color system, and component patterns. They supersede the current geometry-derived layout approach for initial scaffolding. Agents MUST read these files as ground truth for layout structure, then adapt them to the Next.js app-router architecture with dynamic content from the GitHub packet cache.

homepage.description: Landing page / homepage design. Separate design from product pages.

product_page_template.description: Generic product page template for individual ZPE repos (XR, FT, Geo, Ink, IoT, Mocap, Neuro, Prosody, Robotics). Each product page draws ALL content from its GitHub repo. This is an enhanced, visualized GitHub page with placeholder spaces for physics widgets/visualizations of the 8 primitives. DARPA-leak aesthetic.

zpe_imc_page.description: ZPE-IMC flagship page. HYBRID role: (1) its own product story as a codec, AND (2) overarching foundational content/modelling/explanation/context for the repos that sit under it. Must merge the product page template with the IMC-specific design.

content_pipeline.principle: Every product page pulls its content and copy DIRECTLY from the relevant GitHub repo. The Stitch HTML is conceptual layout only — no content guidance was given to Stitch. The agents must wire up the packet cache (site/.cache/packets/) and loadLaneCatalog()/loadLaneBySlug() to populate the Stitch layout shells with real repo data.
content_pipeline.visuals: Use placeholder visuals/boxes for now in spaces where visualizations/widgets are needed. These will be filled later with physics visualizations of the 8 primitives.
content_pipeline.merchandising: There is thinking still to do about how to merchandise content from repos onto pages. For now, map obvious sections (hero, metrics, proof assertions, evidence routes, related lanes) to packet fields and GitHub data.

agent_instructions.product-family: Read the Stitch product page template HTML. Extract the Tailwind layout structure, section ordering, and component patterns. Rebuild /work/[slug]/page.tsx to match this layout, replacing static Stitch content with dynamic packet data from loadLaneBySlug(). The hero geometry, metrics grid, proof badges, evidence routes, repo shape, and related lanes sections should all map to packet cache fields. Keep the DARPA-leak aesthetic. Use placeholder divs for widget/visualization spaces.
agent_instructions.homepage-fidelity: Read the Stitch homepage HTML. This is a SEPARATE design from the product pages. Compare against the current homepage implementation and identify structural divergences. The homepage is already ACCEPTED in the CLAW system, so only flag changes — do not break what works.
agent_instructions.data-truth: Verify that packet cache fields can populate all content sections in the Stitch product page template. Identify any gaps where the packet schema needs new fields to support the Stitch layout.
agent_instructions.systems-qa: After product-family delivers a Stitch-based candidate, run falsification using the Stitch HTML as the layout reference rather than the old geometry specs. The Stitch code IS the design spec.
agent_instructions.integration: The Stitch rescaffold is a layout-level change. Verify that the new layout still passes build, parser tests, and does not regress accepted routes.

scope_notes: The Stitch prototypes use cdn.tailwindcss.com — the Next.js site uses Tailwind via PostCSS. Agents must translate Tailwind classes, not import the CDN.
Stitch uses Google Fonts links — the Next.js site uses next/font. Preserve the Oswald + Courier Prime choices but use the Next.js font loading pattern.
The Stitch img src uses Google CDN URLs — replace with Next.js Image component and placeholder images.
Do NOT discard GGD geometry laws. The Stitch layout becomes the new geometry reference, and laws should be updated to match it rather than the old abstract specs.

brand_assets.usage_notes: Logo SVG must be used directly (not recreated in code) in all prescribed placements (nav header, footer). Copy the SVG into site/public/ and reference via Next.js. Icon SVG is reserved for future creative phases — do not use yet.
